author: John Kanell
description: These Mini Cheesecakes have a crunchy graham cracker pecan crust and a rich, creamy filling that bakes quickly. They're easy to make, perfectly portioned, and ideal for customizing with your favorite toppings.
equipment: Shot glass or small flat measuring cup
ingredients: 1/4 cup sour cream, room temperature (60 g)
instructions: Preheat the oven to 350°F and line a 12-cup muffin pan with paper liners.
Add the graham crackers and toasted pecans to a food processor and pulse until finely crushed. Add the sugar and cinnamon and pulse to combine.
Drizzle 4 tablespoons of the melted butter over the crumbs and pulse until evenly moistened. If the mixture feels dry, add the remaining tablespoon of butter.
Divide the crust mixture evenly among the muffin cups (about 2 packed tablespoons each) and press firmly into an even layer. Bake for 8 to 10 minutes, until fragrant. Set aside to cool slightly.
In a stand mixer, beat the cream cheese on medium speed for 2 to 3 minutes until smooth and creamy.
Add the sugar and salt and mix until fully combined, scraping down the bowl as needed.
Add the sour cream and vanilla extract and mix until smooth. Add the eggs one at a time, mixing on medium-low until just incorporated.
Scoop a scant 1/4 cup of filling into each muffin cup, filling nearly to the top.
Bake for 15 to 17 minutes, until the centers jiggle slightly when shaken.
Cool to room temperature in the pan, then remove and refrigerate until chilled before serving.
notes: If the cheesecakes crack, simply cover with toppings such as whipped cream, fruit, or chocolate sauce.
Store in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
Freeze cooled cheesecakes for up to 3 months and thaw overnight in the refrigerator.
title: Mini Cheesecakes
